title: VBMBC + The HIVE: Spring Small Business Roundtable
description:
**Working Smarter, Not Harder: Scaling Your Business in the Digital World**

​Running a small business today means wearing many hats. The good news is you do not have to do everything the hard way.
​Join the **Virginia Beach** **Minority Business Council** and **The HIVE Small Business Resource Center** for an interactive session designed to help entrepreneurs understand how AI and modern digital tools can simplify operations, save time, and support sustainable growth.
​This session will introduce practical ways small business owners can use AI today. You will learn how automation and digital tools can reduce repetitive work, improve decision making, and help you focus more energy on growing your business.

​**Required registration**
​Registration is required to attend this event.

​Please sign up here:
https://www.eventbrite.com/e/2026-mbc-spring-small-business-roundtable-tickets-1981794826675)

​Attendees who have not registered may be turned away at the event if capacity is reached.

​**What you will learn**
​• What AI actually means for small businesses today
​• Practical digital tools you can begin using immediately
​• How automation can reduce manual work and free up your time
​• How to take advantage of resources already available to entrepreneurs in Hampton Roads
​
Whether you are just beginning to explore digital tools or looking for smarter ways to scale your business, this event focuses on practical strategies that work in today’s fast moving business environment.

​**Featured speaker**
​**Erin Marie**
​Co-Founder & Director @ The AI Collective Hampton Roads
​AI Modernization\, Automation & Strategy Consultant \| Former Microsoft Technical Program Manager

​Erin Marie is a co-founder of **AI Collective Hampton Roads**, a regional community that brings together builders, leaders, and practitioners to explore practical and responsible applications of AI. Through workshops, hackathons, and community events, the organization helps people learn how to apply AI in ways that are useful, accessible, and human centered.
​
Prior to this work, Erin spent five years at Microsoft as a Technical Program Manager leading modernization efforts for the Windows servicing pipeline. Her work focused on system design, distributed computing, and helping organizations modernize workflows through thoughtful adoption of automation and AI.
​She is known for helping technical and non technical teams work together effectively while building systems that support both business outcomes and the people behind them.

**Partnership**
​This event is hosted by **Virginia Beach Minority Business Council & The HIVE Small Business Resource Center of Virginia Beach**, which provides education, mentoring, and resources to help entrepreneurs launch and grow successful businesses.

**Parking**
​Parking is available in the **Red Garage**. Enter on Market Street, walk around the building to **140 Independence Blvd**, and enter through the front entrance.

title: Claude Code: From Setup to Agentic Workflows | AICHR
description:
***This event is capped at 500 attendees. Register now to secure your spot.***

**Claude Code: From Setup to Agentic Workflows**
Hands-on with the tool that's turning developers and power users into one-person engineering teams.

*This is a live, digital-only event. No in-person attendance. Join from anywhere.*

*April 9, 2026 · 6:30 PM EST*

AI coding assistants have been around for a while now. Most of them autocomplete your lines and call it a day. Claude Code is something different — it's an agentic system that lives in your terminal, reads your entire codebase, executes multi-file operations, and coordinates work across sessions and sub-agents.

This session is a live, demo-driven walkthrough of Claude Code and Anthropic's expanding agentic toolkit. We're not talking theory. We're opening terminals, pairing devices, and building things in real time.
Whether you're a developer looking to 10x your workflow or a technical professional who wants to understand what agentic coding actually looks like in practice, this is the session to attend.

Join Andrew Stafford for a hands-on tour of how Claude's tools work together to turn a single prompt into a coordinated, multi-step workflow.

🔍 **What We'll Cover**

**Claude Code: Setup & Core Concepts** Installation, configuration, and your first agentic session. How Claude Code reads your codebase, plans changes, and executes across files — not just autocomplete, but autonomous multi-step workflows.

**Tasks: From To-Dos to Orchestration** How Claude Code's Task system manages complex work with dependency graphs, filesystem persistence, and cross-session coordination. Break down big projects into parallelizable work that survives session restarts.

**Cowork: Agentic Power Beyond Code** Claude Code's capabilities applied to knowledge work on your desktop. Local file access, connectors to Slack, Notion, Google Drive, and more — all running in a sandboxed VM on your machine. Live demo of real workflow automation.

**Dispatch: Your Phone as a Remote Control** The newest feature in the toolkit. Pair your phone to your desktop Cowork session, fire off tasks from anywhere, and come back to finished work. We'll set it up live and put it through its paces.

**The Agentic Stack: How It All Fits Together** Claude Code in the terminal. Cowork on the desktop. Dispatch from your phone. Tasks coordinating across sessions. Sub-agents working in parallel. We'll map out how these pieces connect into a unified workflow and where they're heading.

🎙️ **Your Speaker**
Andrew Stafford Co-Lead of The AI Collective Hampton Roads 25+ years across technology, cloud architecture, and AI — including work with Amazon, NASA, healthcare platforms, and enterprise systems. Author of *AI for Business*. Currently building at the intersection of AI tooling, agentic workflows, and practical business automation.

Also runs [Claude Code for Business Group](https://www.facebook.com/groups/claudecodeforbusiness)
And [AI Consulting Group](https://fractionalcaio.org/)

**Who should attend?**

* Developers and engineers who want to go beyond copilot-style autocomplete
* Technical professionals curious about agentic AI workflows
* Founders and operators looking to understand the productivity shift
* Anyone who builds, ships, or manages software and wants to see what's possible now

**Why this matters**
The gap between "AI assistant" and "AI agent" is closing fast. Claude Code doesn't just suggest code — it plans, executes, verifies, and coordinates. Cowork extends that to knowledge work. Dispatch makes it mobile. Tasks make it persistent.

This isn't a future roadmap talk. These tools are live, shipping, and available today. The question isn't whether agentic workflows will change how you work — it's how fast you'll adopt them.

Come see what it looks like when you stop chatting with AI and start delegating to it.

title: Quantum Computing with Q# and Azure Quantum
description:
**Quantum Computing with Q# and Azure Quantum**

Quantum Computing is a rising field in Computer Science that uses the effects of Quantum Mechanics to achieve computations that are very difficult to do or simply impossible to do with regular computer technology. It can be used to simulate complex molecules for research, find optimal solutions for complex datasets, or could eventually break RSA encryption with sufficiently powerful quantum computers. To enter the Quantum Computing field, Microsoft created Q#, a domain specific language for performing quantum computations and Azure Quantum, an Azure service that provides cloud access to quantum simulators and selected real quantum computers.

* 6:00 PM — Food, drinks, and networking

title: John Morehouse: Exploring Optimized Locking in SQL Server 2025
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!!**
Exploring Optimized Locking in SQL Server 2025

When updating data in SQL Server, operators run the gambit of incurring issues if not done correctly. Sometimes, they manifested as lock escalation, the dreaded blocking, disastrous deadlocks, or poor performance. With the release of Optimized Locking, first seen in Azure SQL Database and now in SQL Server 2025, issues are mitigated while helping to enhance performance and transactional concurrency.

In this session, we'll embark on a journey into this new capability, designed to revolutionize concurrency control and improve transaction throughput. We'll examine the underlying mechanism while providing insights into its architecture, implementation, and best practices for maximizing performance.

You will -
• Learn how optimized locking works
• Discover what it requires to function
• Some Gotchas to be aware of

Whether you're a seasoned database professional or a curious developer, this session offers a more profound understanding of SQL Servers's optimized locking feature.
group: Hampton Roads SQL Server User Group

title: Claude Code Agent Teams Workshop | AICHR
description:
***This event is capped at 500 attendees. Register now to secure your spot.***

You've set up Claude Code. You've built your first automations. Now it's time to scale.
Agent Teams is Claude Code's most powerful feature — multiple AI instances working in parallel, sharing task lists, and coordinating directly with each other. Instead of one agent handling everything sequentially, you spin up a team: frontend, backend, QA — each working independently while a team lead orchestrates the whole operation.
This session is a live, demo-driven deep dive into Agent Teams. We're not talking theory. We're spinning up a real multi-agent team on stage and building a project in parallel — live.
If you attended our April 9 event (or watched the recording), this is the next step. If you didn't, you can still follow along — but having Claude Code installed will help.
Join Andrew Stafford for a hands-on look at how to go from "one AI assistant" to "an AI workforce."

🔍 **What We'll Cover**

**Agent Teams Setup** The one-line configuration that unlocks multi-agent orchestration. tmux for split-pane visibility. Display modes. Permission management.

**Live Demo: Multi-Agent Build** We'll spin up a 3-agent team — two builders and a reviewer — and build a real feature in parallel. You'll watch the agents claim tasks, communicate with each other, and coordinate their work in real time.

**Prompt Engineering for Teams** How to define roles, assign file ownership, set communication protocols, and prevent the #1 failure mode (agents overwriting each other's work).

**Plan-Then-Execute at Scale** The pattern that makes Agent Teams reliable: every agent drafts a plan, gets approval, then executes. No wasted tokens, no wrong-direction builds.

**When to Use Teams vs Sub-Agents vs Solo** Agent Teams aren't always the answer. We'll cover the decision framework for choosing the right approach based on your task complexity and budget.

**Token Economics** Agent Teams use 3-5x the tokens of a single session. We'll break down the real costs and show you how to optimize for value.

title: Claude as Your AI Coworker | AICHR
description:
***This event is capped at 500 attendees. Register now to secure your spot.***

Everyone thinks Claude Code is for developers. It's not.
Claude Code and Cowork are the closest things we have to a true AI coworker — one that connects to your email, your calendar, your files, and your apps, then actually does the work instead of just answering questions about it.
This session shows you 10 practical things Claude can do for you right now — no coding required. Every example follows the same pattern: you provide the idea and make the final call, the AI handles the grunt work, and you review before anything goes live. Human in the loop, every step.
This is our most accessible event yet. If you've been curious about AI but felt like everything was aimed at developers — this one's for you.

🔍 **What We'll Cover**

**10 Live Demonstrations (or as many as we can get done in the hour)**

1. **Daily Morning Briefing** — Calendar + email + priorities, generated before you wake up
2. **Meeting Prep Researcher** — Topic research + one-page briefing, ready before you walk in
3. **Email Triage Assistant** — Scan inbox, flag priorities, draft replies for your review
4. **File Organizer** — 500 messy files → sorted, renamed, organized in minutes
5. **Data Cleanup Specialist** — Messy spreadsheets → clean, standardized, formatted
6. **Competitive Research Analyst** — 5 competitors → structured landscape document
7. **Weekly Status Report Generator** — Compile your week's work into a summary
8. **Travel Planner** — Budget + dates + preferences → complete itinerary
9. **Subscription Audit** — Find and flag every unused recurring charge
10. **Presentation Generator** — Notes or a topic → branded slide deck

**The Human-in-the-Loop Pattern** Every demo follows the same principle: you bring the starting ideas and judgment. The AI does the heavy lifting. You review and approve before anything is final. The AI is the intern — you're the boss.

**Setup for Non-Technical People** We'll show you how to get Cowork running on your machine — no terminal, no code, no developer tools. Just the Claude Desktop app and your existing accounts.

title: 670K people watched us vibe code
description:
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qYx8NX49OS8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qYx8NX49OS8)
Imagine being flown across the country to build a full-stack app from scratch in under four hours, where you have to demo what you made in front of a dev influencer, corporate recruiter, and competing engineering teams regardless of if it's finished or a broken mess.

Now imagine that it's all being filmed by a full production crew, having to juggle team interviews and makeup touchups between code commits and debugging attempts, all to make a video that's been viewed over 670,000 times! Terrifying right?

Ryan McGovern and Austin Galyon did exactly that while competing in a CodeTV Web Dev Challenge sponsored by Intuit, where they built Pantry Party - a collaborative generative recipe app built using Astro, Convex, Clerk, Netlify, and the Claude API, most of which they had never seen or used before that morning. They'll share what the experience was actually like behind the scenes, where AI-assisted coding helped, where it completely failed, and how their tooling has evolved since.

All skill levels welcome! The [757Dev Community](757dev.org) means a lot to me, and I thank each of you for your support. 🖖

As always, HUGE thanks to Assembly for hosting. Learn more about the amenities and community of Assembly at [https://www.assemblynfk.com/](https://www.assemblynfk.com/)

Street parking is free after 6 pm. From 6:30-7:00 pm, we will have someone at the door to let you in. After 7, message the #javascript channel on 757Dev Slack. We meet in the Fur Room on the 4th floor.

title: Building an AI RAG Application with Logic Apps
description:
*PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE NOTE THE DATE. NOT OUR NORMAL SCHEDULE.*

**Building an AI RAG Application with Logic Apps**
We have a special guest in-person this month! Microsoft Azure MVP, Dan Toomey, is visiting us all the way from Australia!

**Schedule**
5:45 PM — Doors open
6:00 PM — Food, drinks, and networking
6:30 PM — Presentation begins

**About This Session**
Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) is a proven model for exposing your data to AI-assisted search platforms whilst breaking through the limitations of unwieldy Large Language Modules (LLMs). Microsoft gives us great tools like Azure AI Search, Azure OpenAI, and OpenAI Studio to build these solutions, but the complexity of the components and how they all fit together can be daunting. In this session, find out how Logic Apps Templates and built-in AI connectors can simplify the construction of both the ingestion and the retrieval processes that comprise your RAG platform, with no coding required!

**Speaker**
Dan Toomey is a Senior Integration Specialist for Deloitte as well as a Microsoft Azure MVP, a Microsoft Certified Azure Solutions Architect (MCSE), a Microsoft Certified Azure Developer (MCPD), and a Microsoft Certified Technical Specialist (MCTS) in BizTalk Server 2010. He’s also a Microsoft Certified Trainer (MCT) and a published Pluralsight author. Dan is an international speaker, invited to present at global events such as INTEGRATE (London & USA), Aviators in Hamburg (Germany), and PolarConf (Finland), as well as many local Australian events such as Microsoft Ignite Australia (Sydney), API Days (Melbourne), the Global Azure Bootcamp and Xamarin Dev Days. He founded the Brisbane BizTalk User Group in 2005 and is co-organiser of the Brisbane Azure User Group. Dan has authored two courses for Pluralsight and is an active blogger [[http://www.mindovermessaging.com\]](http://www.mindovermessaging.com%5D/).

**Location**
Marathon Consulting (2nd Floor)
Plenty of free parking is available behind the building. If you're arriving later, the doors may be locked—check the sign on the door for instructions on how to get in.

**Details**
Food and drinks will be provided. This event is free and open to all skill levels.

title: Kevin Feasel- Data Virtualization in SQL Server
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!!**
SQL Server's data virtualization stack has evolved throughout the past decade, allowing you to integrate SQL Server with a variety of external systems, such as Oracle, MongoDB, Cosmos DB, Azure Blob Storage, and AWS S3. In this talk, we will briefly cover the history of data virtualization in SQL Server and focus on how we can write T-SQL queries to access data from heterogeneous sources, both in SQL Server 2025 and in prior versions. Finally, we will look at several interesting use cases for data virtualization in the modern data environment.

SQL Server 2025 is the AI database for enterprises. But there are more new things than just AI—in this demo filled, full-day workshop, you will learn about:

• Engine improvements that make everything just run faster
• How SQL Server 2025 helps you operate more securely
• Further enhancements to high availability
• Building event-driven architectures with change event streaming

Master the benefits of the latest release of SQL Server and how to deploy it in your organization.

title: Cybersecurity - Chesapeake Weekend Afternoon Networking (WAN)
description:
**Attention Information Security and Cybersecurity Professionals!**
Are you looking to meet new people who share your interests? Whether you're thinking about entering cybersecurity, have recently entered the field, or are a seasoned professional, we welcome all to join us for an evening of networking, learning, and fun.
**Why Attend?**
Enjoy a night out at the cozy and welcoming **Neat Bird** in Chesapeake, where you can connect with like-minded individuals. We love bringing people together to share insights and build lasting connections. Join us for some beers—or not, if that's your preference. Our goal is to help you forge meaningful relationships in the cybersecurity community.
**About ISSA-HR**
The Information Systems Security Association of Hampton Roads (ISSA-HR) hosts social events throughout the region, providing a space for cybersecurity professionals and enthusiasts to get together, share stories, learn new information, and meet new people. With years of cybersecurity expertise, ISSA-HR is committed to promoting the industry and nurturing the next generation of cybersecurity professionals.

**RSVP now and bring a friend!** We look forward to seeing you there!

**Location:** Neat Bird, 565 Belaire Ave, Chesapeake, VA 23320
Time: **6:00 - 8:00 PM**
group: Information Systems Security Association ISSA Hampton Roads

title: 🧜‍♀️ AICHR | Connections & (grocery) carts
description:
Meet people and pick up your weekly groceries.

🤝 **A casual networking hangout in the grocery store cafe**
Chats, connections, & grocery carts is a relaxed, social-first networking event for curious, friendly people across Hampton Roads who enjoy good conversation and knocking out life errands at the same time.

This is not a lecture.
This is not a pitch night.
And you definitely do not need to “prepare” anything.

It is simply a low-pressure space to meet new people, have real conversations, and casually connect while wandering the aisles and picking up your weekly groceries.

Whether you are deeply into AI and automation, adjacent to tech and creativity, or just someone who enjoys thoughtful conversations with interesting humans, you belong here.

✨ **What to expect**
\- Casual\, open networking with a mix of builders\, creatives\, technologists\, founders\, students\, and professionals
\- Easy\, organic conversations while shopping at your own pace
\- Light\, optional conversation prompts to help break the ice if you want them
\- No presentations\, no panels\, no pressure to “network correctly”
\- A friendly\, welcoming environment for all backgrounds and experience levels
\- Come as you are\. Grab a cart or a basket\. Pick up dinner for the week\. \- \- Meet a few new people along the way\.

👥 **Who should attend**
Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts is for:

People who enjoy meeting others without forced networking vibes
Builders, creatives, technologists, and curious minds
AI, automation, no-code, and tech-adjacent folks
Students and career switchers exploring what is next
Founders, consultants, operators, and professionals
Anyone who likes good conversation and low-stakes socializing

If you enjoy thoughtful chats and meeting people in unexpected places, you will fit right in.

🏪 **Why a grocery store**
You were probably going to the store anyway.

Hosting this event in a grocery store keeps things human, practical, and approachable. It removes the pressure of traditional networking and turns it into something simple, efficient, and a little fun.

Two errands. One cart.

💡 **Why this event exists**
Community grows best in relaxed, everyday spaces.

Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts exists to create an easy, human-centered way for people to connect without titles, talks, or expectations. It is about building relationships, sharing ideas naturally, and strengthening the local community one conversation at a time.

Come say hi. Leave with groceries. Make a few new connections along the way.

**ℹ️ Other bits of info & FAQs**

* This is a free community event
* Drinks or food are available for purchase depending on the venue
* Photos or videos may be taken for community and marketing purposes
* Come solo or bring a friend
* ​​​Is there free parking in the area? Yes. They’ve got lots of spots available in their parking lot.
* ​​​Is this free? Yes, but space is limited. Make sure to RSVP.
